focus: [D2-5/D2] /design:* failure modes/preconditions unnamed (md-gen needs Playwright+URL; audit needs concrete target/evidence; motion needs component/interaction)

graphEvents.label: Design command wrappers do not expose mode preconditions or failure modes
md-generator requires live renderable URL plus Node and Playwright readiness
audit requires a resolvable target artifact and evidence labels before strong claims
motion requires target component or expected motion behavior plus budget constraints
impeccable command docs make pitfalls and alternatives first-class
commandSurface preconditions and failureModes can generate wrapper gates and replay fixtures

label: /design:* wrappers expose no precondition or failure-mode contract
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE for docs and metadata presence; ADVISORY for ambiguous runtime interpretation
recommendation: Add commandSurface.preconditions and failureModes, then generate Requires, Ask first if missing, Cannot run when, and Escalate if sections in every design wrapper.

label: /design:md-generator hides hard URL and Playwright prerequisites
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE statically and for local tool preflight; URL reachability remains external until tested
recommendation: Give md-generator concrete URL/output grammar and explicit Requires/Cannot run/Escalate clauses for live renderable URL, Node/Playwright readiness, output path, and extraction failures.

label: /design:audit and /design:motion hide missing-input gates that child modes already define
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE on obvious replay fixtures and target-kind detection; ADVISORY for evidence sufficiency and hierarchy judgment
recommendation: Project audit target/evidence and motion component/state/performance gates into pinned command wrappers and fixture prompts.

label: impeccable-main models preconditions and failure modes as public command docs
enforceability: ENFORCEABLE for section presence and non-generic failure language; ADVISORY for editorial quality
recommendation: Borrow the compact command-doc pattern: When ready, If missing, Failure modes/Pitfalls, and safe alternatives, with richer generated docs optional.

label: A pinned command is not ready to apply a mode until required input kind and failure behavior are visible at the command surface.
audit is not built-surface-only; it can review planned interfaces but needs a resolvable target and evidence labeling.

reason: Pinned commands are an execution surface; if they apply a mode before surfacing missing-input gates, under-specified prompts can produce false claims or generic status failures.
